prompt: Design a Fitness Studio Operations & Finance Console to run member services and internal controls in a fully offline environment. The Layui-based web UI presents a role-aware workspace for Administrator, Front Desk, Coach, Finance, and Operations, with menus and action buttons appearing only when permitted. Users sign in with a local username and password (no phone, email, or third‑party login) and land on dashboards tailored to their job: Front Desk records attendance and requests corrections with required reason text; Coaches review schedules and submit adjustment requests; Finance configures budget categories (e.g., “Equipment,” “Payroll,” “Events”) and allocates monthly caps such as $25,000.00 for 03/01/2026–03/31/2026, then tracks commitments that freeze funds once a reimbursement is submitted; Operations and Admin approve or reject reimbursement workflows with clear on-screen status, timestamps, and inline feedback. Reimbursements capture amount, merchant, service period, and attachments; the UI enforces file type and size limits (PDF/JPG/PNG, max 10 MB each, up to 5 files) and blocks duplicate receipt/invoice numbers before submission. Because some studios run on unreliable LANs, the UI includes weak-network adaptation: it reduces polling frequency, switches to lower-frequency sampling for live operational widgets, supports manual reconnect, and provides state compensation so in-progress forms and approval decisions are not lost after a drop. The backend uses ThinkPHP to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Layui frontend, separating presentation from data services while staying entirely on-prem. MySQL persists users, roles, permissions, budgets, fund allocations, commitment freezes, reimbursements, ledger entries, and immutable-style audit trails. RBAC enforces menu/API/data-scope authorization down to location or department where applicable, and every privileged action writes an audit record including operator, time, local IP, and before/after values for permission changes, rule configuration, attendance corrections, and approval nodes; audit search supports date ranges and export to CSV for reconciliation. Account security rules include minimum 12-character passwords with complexity, lockout for 5 failed logins within 15 minutes (30-minute cooldown), device/session management, and a 90-day password rotation policy; sensitive fields are masked in the UI and stored with hashing/encryption at rest. Budget controls raise over-cap alerts at submission time and prevent approvals that would exceed remaining available funds unless an Admin applies an explicit override reason captured in the audit log. Payment and settlement records are modeled as offline settlements (cash/check/terminal batch entry) with partial refunds and exception states such as “unpaid” or “recorded but not confirmed,” ensuring reconciliation can be completed locally without any external payment networks.
